title: César Torres

(born 23 September 1976) is a Colombian professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") [manager](/wiki/Manager_(association_football) "Manager (association football)"), currently in charge of the [Colombia national under-20 team](/wiki/Colombia_national_under-20_football_team "Colombia national under-20 football team").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Colombian football manager

In this [Spanish name](/wiki/Spanish_name "Spanish name"), the first or paternal [surname](/wiki/Surname "Surname") is  *Torres* and the second or maternal family name is  *Ramírez*.

César Torres

**César Fernando Torres Ramírez** (born 23 September 1976) is a Colombian professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") [manager](/wiki/Manager_(association_football) "Manager (association football)"), currently in charge of the [Colombia national under-20 team](/wiki/Colombia_national_under-20_football_team "Colombia national under-20 football team").

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=C%C3%A9sar_Torres&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Torres was born in [Cali](/wiki/Cali "Cali"), and started his career in the youth categories of hometown side [Boca Juniors de Cali](/wiki/Boca_Juniors_de_Cali "Boca Juniors de Cali"). After spending several years in the club, he moved to [Cortuluá](/wiki/Cortulu%C3%A1 "Cortuluá") in 2006 after being named [Alberto Suárez](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Alberto_Su%C3%A1rez "José Alberto Suárez")'s assistant.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

In 2007, Torres was appointed [Nelson Abadía](/wiki/Nelson_Abad%C3%ADa "Nelson Abadía")'s assistant at [Centauros Villavicencio](/wiki/Centauros_Villavicencio "Centauros Villavicencio"), and was later the club's manager for the latter stages of the campaign. He moved back to his assistant role for the 2009 season, after the appointment of [Eduardo Cruz](/w/index.php?title=Eduardo_Cruz_Rodr%C3%ADguez&action=edit&redlink=1 "Eduardo Cruz Rodríguez (page does not exist)") as manager.

In 2012, after the establishment of [Universitario Popayán](/wiki/Universitario_Popay%C3%A1n "Universitario Popayán"), Torres was named their new manager. He left in the following year to work as Cruz's assistant at [Deportes Quindío](/wiki/Deportes_Quind%C3%ADo "Deportes Quindío"), being also an interim manager of the club after Cruz left.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

In January 2014, Torres returned to Universitario, being in charge of the club in the [Categoría Primera B](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_B "Categoría Primera B"). In October 2018, he was named at the helm of [Alianza Petrolera](/wiki/Alianza_Petrolera "Alianza Petrolera"), seriously threatened with relegation in the [Categoría Primera A](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A "Categoría Primera A").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

After avoiding relegation, Torres led Alianza to the semifinals of the [2019 Torneo Finalización](/wiki/2019_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A_season "2019 Categoría Primera A season"), but was sacked on 16 November 2020 after a 1–6 loss to [Millonarios](/wiki/Millonarios_F.C. "Millonarios F.C.").[[4]](#cite_note-4) On 25 May 2021, he was appointed manager of [Jaguares de Córdoba](/wiki/Jaguares_de_C%C3%B3rdoba "Jaguares de Córdoba").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

On 4 May 2022, Jaguares announced that Torres would leave on a mutual agreement at the end of the [Apertura Tournament](/wiki/2022_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A_season "2022 Categoría Primera A season").[[6]](#cite_note-6) Twenty days later he was announced as the new manager of Cortuluá.[[7]](#cite_note-7) On 18 September, he resigned from Cortuluá after going winless for seven games, leaving the club in last place of both the 2022 Finalización tournament and the season's relegation table.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

On 6 March 2023, Torres was appointed as manager of Categoría Primera B club [Bogotá](/wiki/Bogot%C3%A1_F.C. "Bogotá F.C.").[[9]](#cite_note-9) Torres left Bogotá on 25 June 2023, as he was signed for a second spell as manager of Alianza Petrolera.[[10]](#cite_note-10) With Alianza Petrolera being relocated to [Valledupar](/wiki/Valledupar "Valledupar") prior to the start of the [2024 season](/wiki/2024_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A_season "2024 Categoría Primera A season"), Torres became the manager for its successor club [Alianza F.C.](/wiki/Alianza_F.C._(Colombia) "Alianza F.C. (Colombia)")[[11]](#cite_note-11) On 29 March, he left Alianza on a mutual agreement.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

On 2 May 2024, Torres was appointed as manager of the [Colombia national under-20 team](/wiki/Colombia_national_under-20_football_team "Colombia national under-20 football team").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=C%C3%A9sar_Torres&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Honours")]

**Colombia U20**

* [FIFA U-20 World Cup](/wiki/FIFA_U-20_World_Cup "FIFA U-20 World Cup") third place: [2025](/wiki/2025_FIFA_U-20_World_Cup "2025 FIFA U-20 World Cup")[[14]](#cite_note-14)
